Off Calendar Jan-10-2013 Continued To Jan-30-13 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 204

NOTICE OF NEW HEARING DATE AND ORDER ON REISSUANCE (ELDER OR DEPENDENT ADULT ABUSE)
Off Calendar Jan-30-2013 Continued To Jul-31-13 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven Riess for and with Orlando Clark (Petitioner). Ian Greensides for and with Verna Eberhardt (Respondent). Robert Fleming (Witness for Petitioner). Brenda Spann and Darlene Neel (for City Bank) (Witnesses for Respondent).

At 1:40 p.m., the Court stated that this is on calendar for an Elder Abuse Restraining Order and that there is a pending related civil action (CGC-12-524703). The Court sidebar with both counsel off the record.

Back on the record, Mr. Riess stated that the parties have reached a stipulaton to extend the TRO for six months or until the related civil action is resolved and that this petition will be heard if necessary. The Court instructed Mr. Riess to prepare an TRO using the EA110 form with the identical terms from the TRO issued on November 16, 2012. The matter is continued to July 30, 2013. The Court further advised parties that the Elder Abuse calendar will no longer be heard in Dept. 204 after February 15, 2013.

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Court Reporter: Lewis Gillian CSR #12355. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy Henry.

NOTICE OF COURT HEARING (ELDER OR DEPENDENT ADULT ABUSE)
Off Calendar Dec-10-2012 Continued To Jan-09-13 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 204

NOTICE OF NEW HEARING DATE AND ORDER ON REISSUANCE (ELDER OR DEPENDENT ADULT ABUSE)
Off Calendar Jan-09-2013 Continued To Jan-30-13 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Hearing On Motion To Quash Service
Off Calendar Jan-09-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven Riess for and with Orlando Clark (Petitioner). Ian Greensides for Verna Eberhardt (Respondent). Robert G. Fleming (Witness for the Petitioner).

At 2:30 p.m., the Court apologized for the delay to start this matter. The Court Reporter was just assigned to cover.

The Court is inclined to deny the Motion to Quash Service for Lack of Personal Jurisdiction for the reasons stated on the record. Mr. Greensides requsted for exculsion of non-party witness. Mr. Riess argued that for purpose of the Motion, the witness has the right to be here. The Court does not see any basis to exclude the witness. Mr. Greensides presented oral argument to support his motion. After hearing the argument, the Court denied the Motion to Quash Service.

The Court asked counsel on how to proceed with the Elder Abuse case. Mr. Greensides requested for more time to subpoena bank records and argued that it would be prejudice to his client that she was unable to subpoena records until the Court's ruling on her Motion. The Court asked for number of witness and time estimate for the hearing. Mr. Riess has two witnesses and he estimated 30 to 40 minutes. Mr. Greensides has two witnesses (may be more when he gets the bank records) and he estimated 1 1/2 to 2 hours. The Court is inclined to continue his matter once for 2 or 3 weeks for the reason stated on the record and asked parties to confer on a new hearing date. The parties conferred off the record. Back on the record and Verna Eberhardt (Respondent) is present. Both counsel agreed to continue to January 30, 2013. The Court continued the Request for Restraining Order to January 30, 2013 at 1:30 p.m. in Dept. 204. TRO is extended to the same date. The Court encourged Mr. Greensides to streamline his case. The Court instructed Mr. Riess to prepare a Notice of New Hearing Date and Order on Reissuance. No further service of the order is required beyond mailing a copy to Mr. Greensides because both parties are present. Mr. Riess was also instructed to prepare an Order Denying the Motion to Quash Service.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy Anasse.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Verna Eberhardt
Off Calendar Dec-06-2012 Text Ruling

NOTICE OF COURT HEARING (ELDER OR DEPENDENT ADULT ABUSE)
Off Calendar Dec-05-2012 Continued To Jan-09-13 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven Riess for and with Orlando Clark (Petitioner). Ian Greensides specially appearing for Verna Eberhardt (Respondent). Robert Fleming (Attorney-in-Fact for Dr. Clark).

Mr. Greensides advised the Court that he has filed a Motion to Quash Service and it is set for hearing on January 3, 2013 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302. The Court questioned the manner of service. Mr. Riess argued that personal service was satisfied by CCP 415.40 and Welfare and Institution Code 15657.03. Mr. Greensides stated the Motion to Quash Service is for personal jurisdiction. The Court indicated the hearing on the Motion to Quash Service should be held in Dept. 204 because this is an Elder Abuse case. Both counsel agreed to continued the Motion to Quash Service to January 9, 2013 at 1:30 p.m. in Dept. 204. Mr. Riess requested to continue this hearing for Restraining Order to January 9, 2013 and to extend the TRO to the same date. Mr. Greensides stated that they should pick another hearing date on January 9 to allow discovery before the evidentiary hearing for Restraining Order. The Court stated that if the Court finds jurisdiction on January 9, Mr. Greensides can raise the discovery issue at that time. The Court continued the Request for Restraining Order to January 9, 2013 at 1:30 p.m. in Dept. 204 and extended the TRO to the same date. Mr. Riess is to prepare an order extending the TRO.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Sr. Deputy Benton.
